summary: Full-coverage backend + frontend testing of Submit Result / round-robin score submission. 15/15 backend pytest cases passed and frontend e2e flow (login -> /submit-result -> select event/round/match -> submit -> toast + archive refresh) works end-to-end. Route ordering regression is fixed: /weekly-events/with-schedules now resolves correctly without colliding with /weekly-events/{event_id}.
critical_code_review_comments: _apply_solo_wins_delta helper in server.py (~L2959) is defined but never invoked; the submit-score handler calls db.solo_players.update_many directly. Either remove the dead helper or use it consistently. Low priority cleanup.

Validation order in submit_roundrobin_score: range/tie checks (400) happen AFTER the round/court 404 lookup. That is correct for the current tests but means a request with invalid round AND invalid score will surface the 404 first; documenting this as the contract is fine.

RoundRobinScoreSubmit pydantic model has no Field bounds; the >20 / <0 / equal validation is hand-rolled in the handler. Adding ge=0/le=20 on the model would also yield 422s for malformed payloads earlier (current behavior is 400, which the tests assert).
server.py is 3928 lines — well above the 700-line guideline. Should be split into routers (auth, weekly_events, matches, etc.) in a future refactor.
test_credentials: admin@tennis.com / admin123 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md). Non-admin member auto-registered per session as TEST_member_<uuid>@tennis.com / tennis2025.
seed_data_creation: No new seed data created; reused the pre-seeded 2026-05-10 event with 4 solo_players (Sergei Nabatov, Thomas Pallogudis, Francesco Bucello, Eric Pallas). The edit-undo test self-restores the round 1 score so wins remain consistent. Round 1 final state after run: Team A wins 6-2 (re-submitted by member during permission test, then admin self-restore left it at 6-2 from the latest member submission). Round 2 was scored 6-1 by frontend test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_submit_score.py is idempotent and re-runnable. It uses the existing seeded 2026-05-10 event. The edit test flips the result, asserts deltas, then restores. The non-admin permission test re-submits round 1 with score 6-2, which mutates state but stays within valid scores. If you change seed players or remove the event, fixtures will skip/fail with clear messages.
rca of the issue: Root cause (already fixed by main agent before this run): in FastAPI, path routes are matched in registration order. The literal route /weekly-events/with-schedules was registered AFTER /weekly-events/{event_id}, so 'with-schedules' was treated as event_id and 404'd with 'Event not found'. Fix verified: declaring the literal route BEFORE the parametric route resolves it correctly. test_with_schedules_not_treated_as_event_id explicitly asserts 200 + list shape to lock this regression.
